Procedure: Open reduction and internal fixation (ORIF) of right distal radius
Procedure Summary: ORIF of right distal radius using a volar approach. Fracture reduced and stabilized with volar locking plate and locking screws. Fluoroscopy utilized to confirm reduction and hardware placement. Uneventful course.
Surgeon Notes:
Pre-op: Cefazolin 2 g IV given within 30 minutes of incision. Anesthesia: General endotracheal anesthesia. Regional: Supraclavicular single-shot nerve block performed by anesthesia.
Approach: Volar FCR interval. Reduction achieved with K-wire joystick (removed prior to closure).
Implants: 1 x volar locking plate (7-hole); 6 x locking screw 3.5mm.
Grafts: Cancellous bone graft putty 5cc x1 to support volar metaphyseal defect.
Fluoroscopy: 12 minutes total for reduction and hardware confirmation.
Closure: 2-0 Vicryl x2 packs for deep closure; 3-0 Nylon x1 pack for skin. Sterile dressing applied. Tourniquet time 75 minutes. No complications.
